title: Connecting 30 VPCs at Scale
description:
## From Mesh Sprawl to a Hub

An enterprise has **30 VPCs** spread across **multiple AWS accounts** and is adding more each quarter. They need **any-to-any (transitive) routing** between most VPCs, plus a single place to manage routing and to attach their on-premises Site-to-Site VPN. Their current full-mesh **VPC peering** setup has become unmanageable.

Which approach best meets the requirement for transitive routing and centralized management at this scale?

options.text: Replace the mesh with a **Transit Gateway**: attach each VPC (and the VPN) to it as a hub-and-spoke, using a shared TGW with route tables for segmentation.
Keep building **VPC peering** connections between every pair of VPCs; peering supports transitive routing once you add enough connections.
Create a single 'transit VPC' running self-managed router EC2 instances and route all traffic through them.
Connect all 30 VPCs with **AWS PrivateLink** interface endpoints so they can route to each other transitively.

explanation: **AWS Transit Gateway (TGW)** is the canonical solution for connecting many VPCs (and on-prem links) in a **hub-and-spoke** topology with **transitive** routing and a central control point. Each VPC attaches once to the TGW instead of peering with every other VPC, and TGW route tables let you segment which attachments can reach which — and it can be shared across accounts via Resource Access Manager. Option B is fundamentally wrong: **VPC peering is non-transitive** (traffic cannot hop from VPC A to VPC C through VPC B), and a full mesh of N VPCs needs N(N-1)/2 connections — ~435 for 30 VPCs — which is exactly the unmanageable sprawl they're escaping. Option C (self-managed router EC2 'transit VPC') was an older pattern that TGW made obsolete; it adds operational burden, scaling limits, and a single point of failure. Option D misuses **PrivateLink**, which exposes individual *services* (not full network-level routing between VPCs) and is not a transitive routing fabric.

hints.title: Peering's hard limit
hints.body: VPC peering is non-transitive and scales as N-squared. That alone rules it out for 30-plus VPCs needing any-to-any connectivity.

hints.title: Hub-and-spoke at network level
hints.body: When you need transitive routing across many VPCs and on-prem with one place to manage it, think Transit Gateway.
